summary: This brief post promotes O'Neil's Bloomberg column arguing that the concept of 'pre-existing condition' protections should be expanded beyond healthcare. She suggests the principle that people shouldn't be penalized for circumstances beyond their control has applications in many other domains of life, not just medical insurance.
key_points: Pre-existing condition protections in Obamacare should be defended
The concept of protecting people from discrimination based on pre-existing conditions should expand to other areas beyond health
This represents a broader principle about not penalizing people for circumstances outside their control
money_quotes: The problem of pre-existing conditions extends far beyond health.
key_insight: The principle behind healthcare's pre-existing condition protections—that people shouldn't be penalized for factors beyond their control—should be applied more broadly across society.
